title: dendranthema coreanum subsp. littorale
intro: Dendranthema coreanum subsp. littorale, also known as Korean astilbe or littoral Chinese astilbe, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the coastal regions of Korea and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Dendranthema coreanum subsp. littorale is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also employed in traditional Korean medicine to treat various health conditions.
